figures.caption: Fig. 1 Illustration of the optimal stopping boundaries in the (Xt, pt) state space. The optimal policy partitions the space into a continuation region Cc1 and a stopping region Dc1 , separated by the boundary bc1 (x). A sample trajectory of the augmented process is shown, which starts at (X0, p0) and triggers an alarm at the optimal stopping time τ∗ upon hitting the boundary. Additionally, the figure demonstrates the comparative statics: a higher marginal delay penalty (c1 > c2) strictly lowers the optimal stopping threshold, such that bc1 (x) ≤ bc2 (x).

Fig. 2 Constant-SNR Shiryaev model (λ = 0.05, ρ = 1.0). (a) Value functions Vc below the obstacle G(p) = 1 − p; the smooth-fit points p∗(c) (markers) move left and the continuation region shrinks as c increases. (b) The optimal threshold p∗(c) is monotone decreasing in the delay cost rate c, as predicted by Corollary 8.
